narrative.headline: 2019 Honda Pilot: electrical and engine gripes lead the pack
narrative.summary: Electrical system faults are the most-reported concern, with owners describing sudden screen blackouts and audio disruptions while driving. Engine and fuel-system complaints follow closely, centered on unexpected vibration, multiple simultaneous warning lights, and the engine revving high without driver input. A notable share of owners also report the forward collision avoidance system triggering false brake warnings at various speeds.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Owners report loud crackling from the speakers followed by the navigation screen and speedometer screen going completely black, accompanied by a network connection loss message. · 1 fire · 1 crash · 3 injured

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Owners report sudden vibration while driving, with the check engine light and multiple simultaneous warning lights — including hill start assist, power steering, transmission, and others — activating at the same time. · 2 fire · 1 injured

issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: Owners report the engine revving high on its own with no foot on the accelerator, occurring both at low speeds and while the vehicle is stationary in situations such as a school pickup lane. · 1 crash · 1 injured

issues.title: Forward Collision Avoidance
issues.blurb: Owners report the front brake sensor activating and sounding a warning beep erroneously at various speeds, prompting unintended braking. · 3 crash · 2 injured

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: 88 reports (10%), with owners describing the transmission slipping while driving. The highest injury count of any category here (4 injuries) plus 2 crashes.
